## [00:00 - 02:19]

So here's an example of what you might want to say in one of these emails with a loom video. So it might go something like this, Hey John, I realize we've never met. I've been following your brand for a while and this is where you insert their brand name. And I love what you've been doing specifically with XYZ Campaign or with XYZ Influencer. I was checking out your website and I noticed a couple of things that are dramatically decreasing your conversion rate and ultimately decreasing the amount of sales that you're getting. I just recorded a quick video for you specific to insert brand name here that will hopefully help you capitalize on the marketing spend that you're currently spending as well as capitalize on current traffic that you're getting to your website. You can check out the video right here. You link the video and then you say, hey, hope this finds you well. If you have any questions at all, please feel free to reach out. And then one thing we've tested a little bit is throwing in a little line at the very end saying exactly what you do. So it might be, you know, we help dentists scale and get 100 leads a day or we help real estate agents sell their homes faster, whatever that might be, just add a little blurb at the very end, not pitching anything just saying, hey, this is what we do. So that's kind of how you might want to structure an email as far as once you have that loom video, you might structure it that way. So a little bonus tip as well, if you've made it this far, what you want to do as well is send it on multiple platforms. So send it on Facebook, send it on Instagram and send it over email. That way there's a much higher chance that it's actually going to be received and received by the right person. So if you're sending it on Facebook, it can be the same message through Facebook and Instagram and email. And you can send it at the same time as well. More frequently than not, you'll actually get a response from Facebook because they might have a community manager, they might have a marketing person that's managing, you know, their Facebook messages, and they'll see that and they'll say, oh, okay, cool, like we'll send it to our marketing team or thanks for sending that, let's set up a call. More times than not, you'll get a response from the Facebook message as opposed to the email as well. So it's really important to throw the Facebook, Facebook message in there or the Instagram message in there as well as an email. So hope that helps and reach out if you have any questions.
